summary: Phase 7.A.2b (deferred half of Phase 7.A.2: winning fingerprint + WIN_DIGEST golden + win-state reachability test + renderer NPC-sprite extension + N1/N2 cleanup nits) approved as a standalone phase. Phase 7.A.2 (NPC + shop + boss-FSM) was originally split mid-implementation into 7.A.2a (structural, approved at git 764c5b0) and 7.A.2b (this phase) per a phase-update.json that surfaced after an 8000-seed walker probe against the original boss balance (hpMax 40, atk 9, def 5) produced 0 winners; the strategy-planner pass at artifacts/strategy-phase-7-A-2b.md recommended option (3) hand-crafted action log with optional balance and bundled renderer/cleanup work. The implementation took a hybrid path: (a) walker (tools/build-win-log.ts) was strengthened — weapon-priority on NPC purchases (was lowest priority; the walker reached the boss without a weapon equipped in 8000-seed probes), `maybeHealWeakOnly` for floor-10 topoff (preserves strong heals for the boss-fight inner loop where minBase=4 filters skip syringe/nano-repair against the boss's 5+ counter); (b) boss stats were modestly nerfed — hpMax 40→24, atk 9→6, def 5→4 — bringing the win-region into the strengthened walker's reach; (c) the resulting 1217-action sequence was emitted as a frozen Action[] literal into src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts via the new tools/emit-win-log-module.ts (mirrors the SELF_TEST_LOG_100 / SELF_TEST_INVENTORY_LOG self-contained-module pattern from Phase 3 / Phase 6 — no cross-layer import edge from src/sim/** to tools/**). Concrete deliverables landed: (1) src/registries/monsters.ts — boss balance updated (hpMax 24, atk 6, def 4); the change is safe for SIM_DIGEST + INVENTORY_DIGEST because SELF_TEST_LOG_100 (100 actions) and SELF_TEST_INVENTORY_LOG (16 actions) both stay on floor 1 and never spawn the boss. (2) src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts (1267 lines, NEW) — SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS pinning seed="gamma-195" + commitHash="dev0000" + rulesetVersion="phase1-placeholder-do-not-share"; SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG: readonly Action[] of 1217 frozen entries representing a winning scripted run with 1140 moves + 32 attacks + 15 pickups + 11 buys + 9 descents + 8 uses + 2 equips. The log buys upgrades from 11 NPC interactions, descends through floors 1..9, and defeats the boss to aiState 'boss-phase-3'; final HP 12/30, boss HP 0. (3) src/core/self-test.ts — WIN_DIGEST = fb36a2fe54e3581a6105ed0ef80afcf8269fc5f97ba633612028c54039828447; new 'win-cross-runtime-digest' self-test entry asserts (a) outcome === 'won' and (b) state-hash matches WIN_DIGEST byte-for-byte. (4) src/render/canvas.ts (carry-forward N3) — extended drawScene to iterate state.floorState.npcs (NEW draw block under monsters layer) and to resolve the boss kind to its dedicated atlas slot 'monster.boss.black-ice-v0' (regular monsters fall back to 'monster.ice.daemon'). New constants SLOT_MONSTER_BOSS + KIND_MONSTER_BOSS. Layering documented: tiles → items → NPCs → monsters → player. (5) src/sim/turn.ts (carry-forwards N1, N2) — N1: removed npcInventoryAdd / npcInventoryRemove pass-through wrappers (the misleading 'circular import' doc-comment; no circular import existed); call sites now invoke inventoryAdd / inventoryRemove from src/sim/inventory directly. N2: renamed _shopNextNpcs / _shopNpcsTouched → shopNextNpcs / shopNpcsTouched (the underscore prefix conventionally signals 'intentionally unused'; these variables were used). (6) tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (NEW, 7 tests) — pins the win-state reachability + replay-determinism invariant per docs/PHASES.md:520-525: outcome === 'won', boss hp === 0, replay byte-equality across two runs from genesis, state hash matches WIN_DIGEST, prefix replays are deterministic at 100/600/last-1 action positions, log includes ≥1 BUY action (proves shop interaction), log includes exactly 9 DESCEND actions (proves floors 1..9 descent). (7) tests/render/canvas.test.ts — added 3 NPC-render tests (NPC drawing, boss sprite slot resolution, fallback for non-boss monsters); stub manifest extended with npc.fixer/npc.info-broker/monster.boss sprites. (8) tests/render/render-readonly.test.ts — stub manifest extended (mirror of canvas test stub) so the read-only architectural test passes against current floor states (which now contain Phase 7 NPC kinds). (9) tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts — WIN_DIGEST literal mirrored at top of file per Phase 4 addendum N12 mirror invariant; the cross-runtime browser self-test will surface any drift via the existing __SELF_TEST_DETAILS__ window flag. (10) tools/build-win-log.ts — walker strengthened: weapon-priority NPC-purchase ordering (1.weapon → 2.heals → 3.cyberware was 1.heals → 2.cyberware → 3.weapons), maybeHealWeakOnly floor-10 topoff. Default seed updated to 'gamma-195'. (11) tools/emit-win-log-module.ts (NEW) — one-shot tool that runs buildWinLog and emits src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts as a self-contained frozen-literal module. Re-emit: `npx tsx tools/emit-win-log-module.ts > src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts`. (12) tools/probe-seeds.ts — env-driven SEED_PREFIX/SEED_START/SEED_COUNT + per-floor-10 verbose logging. (13) tools/trace-seed.ts (NEW) — diagnostic tool that runs buildWinLog for a chosen seed and prints player position, boss state, equipment, inventory, action counts, and last 30 actions. Code-reviewer verdict APPROVE WITH NITS with 0 blockers (artifacts/code-review-phase-7-A-2b.md); the only landing-blocking suggestion S1 (stale body docstring in src/render/canvas.ts:149 saying 'tiles → items → monsters → player' but code now does 'tiles → items → NPCs → monsters → player') was addressed in this approval pass — body docstring updated to match header docstring. Three non-blocking suggestions carry forward: S2 (tests/sim/boss-fsm.test.ts + tests/sim/turn.test.ts use synthetic boss fixtures with stale registry stats 40/9/5; tests still pass because they construct Monster directly without registry — cosmetic carry-forward to a future drift sweep); S3 (probe/trace tools have no automated tests — acceptable per the established Phase 7.A.2a pattern for tools/build-win-log.ts); S4 (tools/build-win-log.ts has no smoke test — same rationale). All gates green: 970 tests passing across 69 test files (was 960/68 in 7.A.2a; +10 net-new tests = +7 win-replay + +3 canvas NPC/boss); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build 115.99 KB raw / 33.49 KB gzipped (~55% headroom under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget; +~1.1 KB gzipped from Phase 7.A.2a's 96.40/32.37 — driven by the 1267-line frozen Action[] literal). Coverage thresholds met: src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts = 100/100/100/100; src/sim aggregate = 98.33/91.63/100/98.33 (≥ 95/95/100/85 thresholds); src/render/canvas.ts = 97.12/93.75/100/97.12; src/registries/** = 100/100/100/100. The WIN_DIGEST mirror invariant is preserved across exactly 3 sites: src/core/self-test.ts (the canonical golden), tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts (Phase 4 addendum N12 mirror), and src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts (the doc-comment header for traceability). Phase 7.B (live deploy + cross-runtime Playwright + cross-OS atlas-equality matrix) is unblocked.
suggested_commit_message: phase-7.A.2b: WIN_DIGEST + win-state reachability + renderer NPC sprites + N1/N2 cleanup; boss balance hpMax 40→24 atk 9→6 def 5→4 (SIM_DIGEST/INVENTORY_DIGEST preserved)

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 7.A.2b's WIN_DIGEST + reachability test + renderer extension + N1/N2 cleanup maintain prior-phase invariants (SIM_DIGEST + INVENTORY_DIGEST + RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST preserved; existing 29 atlas sprite coordinates byte-identical from Phase 7.A.2a; renderer remains a read-only sink on sim state) and that every gate is green at the time of phase approval.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 970 passed across 69 test files. Net-new vs Phase 7.A.2a (960 across 68): +10 (win-replay.test.ts +7, canvas.test.ts +3 for NPC/boss/fallback render tests). All passing. The new win-cross-runtime-digest self-test entry runs in both Node and (on Phase 7.B's live deploy) browser environments against a single golden WIN_DIGEST, mirroring the SIM_DIGEST and INVENTORY_DIGEST cross-runtime patterns.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The renamed shopNextNpcs / shopNpcsTouched variables and the inlined inventoryAdd / inventoryRemove call sites comply with the existing src/sim/** lint scope. The new src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts comply with all sim-layer scope rules (no float arithmetic, no Math.random, no Date, no Buffer / node:buffer / crypto). The new tools/emit-win-log-module.ts and tools/trace-seed.ts comply with the tools/** node:-prefix import discipline.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. The new WIN_DIGEST constant + win-cross-runtime-digest self-test entry + SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS / SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG type-check against the existing FingerprintInputs and Action[] types. The renderer's new NPC iteration block typechecks against state.floorState.npcs: readonly FloorNpc[] and the boss-kind dispatch typechecks against the MonsterKindId discriminator.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 115.99 KB raw / 33.49 KB gzipped. Delta from Phase 7.A.2a (96.40 / 32.37): +19.59 KB raw / +1.12 KB gzipped — overwhelmingly driven by the 1267-line src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts frozen Action[] literal (the raw delta is the literal entries; the gzipped delta is small because the Action[] entries compress well). ~55% gzipped headroom under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget.
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible regressions on the existing diagnostic + playable + inventory surfaces. The renderer now visually surfaces NPCs (npc.fixer, npc.info-broker, npc.ripperdoc as their dedicated sprites; previously unrendered — code-reviewer N3) and the boss (monster.boss.black-ice-v0 as its dedicated sprite; previously rendered as the generic monster.ice.daemon fallback). The cross-runtime Playwright suite from Phases 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B/5.B/6.B/7.A.1 will keep passing on the unchanged ATLAS_DIGEST + preset-seed values from Phase 7.A.2a (the atlas binary itself is unchanged in 7.A.2b). The new win-screen panel from Phase 7.A.2a is now reachable: when SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG runs to completion, state.outcome === 'won' triggers the panel display. Phase 7.B will assert the win-replay end-to-end on the live deployment and re-run the cross-OS atlas-equality matrix on the existing 1610-byte atlas binary.

winning_fingerprint_pinned.evidence: src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts (1267 lines): SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS = { commitHash: 'dev0000', rulesetVersion: 'phase1-placeholder-do-not-share', seed: 'gamma-195', modIds: [] }. SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG = readonly Action[] of 1217 frozen entries (verified by tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts: action counts 1140 moves + 32 attacks + 15 pickups + 11 buys + 9 descents + 8 uses + 2 equips). The chosen seed reaches floor 10, defeats the boss (hp 0), and ends with outcome === 'won' at player HP 12/30 — verified by the new win-cross-runtime-digest self-test entry and by 7 win-replay tests.

win_digest_golden_pinned_and_mirrored.evidence: WIN_DIGEST = fb36a2fe54e3581a6105ed0ef80afcf8269fc5f97ba633612028c54039828447 in src/core/self-test.ts:121-122. Mirrored in tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts:9-21 (Phase 4 addendum N12 mirror invariant; the literal const + `void WIN_DIGEST` participates in the linted mirror invariant; the cross-runtime browser assertion is via the existing __SELF_TEST_DETAILS__ surface that Phase 7.B's Playwright job already consumes). Doc-comment reference in src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts:18 (the generator-output traceability header).

win_state_reachability_test.evidence: tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (7 tests): (1) outcome === 'won' on floor 10 with player HP > 0; (2) boss is defeated (HP 0) when outcome === 'won'; (3) replay byte-equality — re-running the log twice from genesis produces byte-identical state hashes; (4) state hash matches the pinned WIN_DIGEST; (5) prefix replays are deterministic at 100 / midpoint / last-1 action positions; (6) the log contains ≥1 BUY action (proves shop interaction); (7) the log contains exactly 9 DESCEND actions (proves floors 1..9 descent).

win_cross_runtime_digest_self_test.evidence: src/core/self-test.ts: new self-test entry 'win-cross-runtime-digest' runs runScripted({ inputs: SELF_TEST_WIN_INPUTS, actions: SELF_TEST_WIN_LOG }) and asserts (a) result.outcome === 'won' with explicit error message; (b) sha256Hex(result.finalState.stateHash) === WIN_DIGEST. The entry runs in both Node (via the existing src/core/self-test.test.ts surface) and the browser (via src/main.ts's diagnostic surface; Phase 7.B's cross-runtime Playwright job will assert green). Surfaces a cross-runtime drift on any silent change to the talk/buy/sell handlers, boss FSM phase transitions, shop-stock / shop-price roll domains, or boss-room spawn override.

renderer_npc_and_boss_sprite_extension.evidence: src/render/canvas.ts: drawScene now iterates state.floorState.npcs (new draw block under the monsters layer) and resolves boss kind to monster.boss.black-ice-v0 atlas slot (was previously rendered as the generic monster.ice.daemon fallback). Body docstring updated to document the new layering: tiles → items → NPCs → monsters → player. tests/render/canvas.test.ts gains 3 tests covering: (a) every NPC at its (x, y) cell receives a drawImage call; (b) the boss's dedicated sprite slot (12, 1) at sx=204 sy=17 is sourced for the boss; (c) non-boss monsters use the monster.ice.daemon fallback at sx=51 sy=0. tests/render/render-readonly.test.ts stub manifest extended with the new sprites so the read-only architectural test continues to pass against current floor states. Code-reviewer N3 closed: the live 7.B deploy will visually surface NPC kinds + the boss instead of unrendered npc.fixer / npc.info-broker / monster.boss.black-ice-v0 cells.

n1_pass_through_wrappers_removed.evidence: src/sim/turn.ts: removed the npcInventoryAdd / npcInventoryRemove pass-through wrappers (formerly at lines 262-277). The misleading 'circular import' doc-comment was inaccurate — no circular import exists between src/sim/turn.ts and src/sim/inventory.ts. Call sites at the buy/sell handlers now invoke inventoryAdd / inventoryRemove directly from src/sim/inventory. Behavior unchanged; all 970 unit tests pass including the 12 shop tests in tests/sim/shop.test.ts.

n2_underscore_prefix_rename.evidence: src/sim/turn.ts: _shopNextNpcs / _shopNpcsTouched renamed to shopNextNpcs / shopNpcsTouched. The underscore prefix conventionally signals 'intentionally unused' which was misleading — these variables ARE used (the shop write-staging mechanism). Behavior unchanged; tests pass.

boss_balance_change_safe_for_existing_di.evidence: Boss stats updated in src/registries/monsters.ts: hpMax 40→24, atk 9→6, def 5→4. The change is safe for the existing cross-runtime digest goldens because: (1) SELF_TEST_LOG_100 is a 100-action log running on floor 1 (player never descends to floor 10 → boss never spawned → SIM_DIGEST unaffected); (2) SELF_TEST_INVENTORY_LOG is a 16-action log running on floor 1 (boss never spawned → INVENTORY_DIGEST unaffected); (3) RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST is a pure PRNG walk over the state chain (no boss involvement); (4) MAPGEN_DIGEST is over floors 1..10 ASCII serialization with the boss arena layout — the boss STATS are runtime-only and not part of the mapgen output (verified by re-running self-test.test.ts: all 4 cross-runtime digests still match their goldens). The boss-stat change is implicit ruleset-version bump via the RULES_FILES content change → derivedRulesetVersion change at build time.

self_contained_module_pattern.evidence: src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts is a self-contained frozen-literal module (no imports from tools/). Mirrors the established SELF_TEST_LOG_100 / SELF_TEST_INVENTORY_LOG patterns from Phase 3 / Phase 6: the module imports only Action / FingerprintInputs types from src/core and the action-type / direction constants from src/sim/params. The module is regeneratable via tools/emit-win-log-module.ts (one-shot tool with reproducibility-asserting check that verifies the log wins before emitting); re-emit: `npx tsx tools/emit-win-log-module.ts > src/sim/self-test-win-log.ts`. Code-reviewer verified the emit tool is byte-identical to the checked-in module.

code_review.evidence: artifacts/code-review-phase-7-A-2b.md: verdict APPROVE WITH NITS, 0 blockers. All 9 review focus items pass: determinism, WIN_DIGEST mirror invariant, boss balance change safety, renderer N3 correctness, N1 cleanup correctness, N2 cleanup correctness, self-contained module pattern, test sufficiency, bundle budget. The single landing-blocking-by-cosmetic suggestion S1 (stale body docstring in src/render/canvas.ts:149 said 'tiles → items → monsters → player' but the new draw order is 'tiles → items → NPCs → monsters → player') was addressed in this approval pass — body docstring updated to match the header. Three non-blocking suggestions carry forward to a future drift sweep: S2 (boss-fsm.test.ts + turn.test.ts synthetic fixtures use stale 40/9/5 stats; tests pass because synthetic state bypasses the registry); S3 (probe/trace tools have no tests); S4 (build-win-log.ts has no smoke test).

follow_ups_carried_forward: S2 (drift sweep candidate) — tests/sim/boss-fsm.test.ts:47-48 and tests/sim/turn.test.ts:419-447 construct synthetic Monster fixtures with the OLD boss stats (hpMax: 40, atk: 9, def: 5). Tests still pass because the fixtures bypass src/registries/monsters.ts. A future drift sweep that touches these test files should update the fixtures to mirror the registry's new (hpMax 24, atk 6, def 4) values for documentation purposes — the tests' actual invariants (FSM phase transitions on integer HP/hpMax thresholds) are HP-fraction-driven and don't depend on absolute stat values.

S3 — tools/probe-seeds.ts and tools/trace-seed.ts are operational diagnostic tools with no automated test coverage. Acceptable per the established Phase 7.A.2a pattern (tools/build-win-log.ts is also untested). If these tools become load-bearing for Phase 8 fingerprint-replay verification, add smoke tests at that boundary.

S4 — tools/build-win-log.ts has no smoke test. Same rationale as S3. The walker is exercised end-to-end via tools/emit-win-log-module.ts (which asserts buildWinLog wins before emitting) and via the win-replay test which consumes its emitted output.

Phase 7.B will need to re-pin __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ - the current diagnostic page only surfaces SIM_DIGEST. Phase 7.B should add an explicit __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ window flag and an e2e test that asserts equality against WIN_DIGEST in chromium / firefox / webkit (mirroring the existing scripted-playthrough → __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ → SIM_DIGEST pattern).
follow_ups_observations: Phase 7.A.2 was the FIRST phase in the project's history split mid-implementation due to a discovered acceptance-criterion gap (winning fingerprint reachability) rather than a planning-gate boundary. The 7.A.2a (structural) + 7.A.2b (reachability + cleanup) decomposition is now an established pattern for future phases that surface acceptance-criterion gaps after the structural code is in place.

The hybrid approach (walker strengthening + balance tuning + emit-as-frozen-literal) was strictly necessary: option (1) walker-strengthening alone produced 0 winners across 8000+ probes; option (2) balance-tuning alone with the original walker produced 0 winners across multiple probed configurations; option (3) hand-crafted log alone would have required ~1200 entries of manual authoring per the strategy memo. The hybrid path uses the strengthened walker as a synthesizer + emits its output deterministically, mirroring the SELF_TEST_LOG_100 generator pattern.

Bundle size delta (+1.12 KB gzipped) is dominated by the 1217-action frozen literal. At ~55% headroom under the 75 KB Phase 2.0 budget, there's room for the WIN_DIGEST surface to grow over time (e.g. multiple winning fingerprints for different boss balance variants in Phase 9 polish).

Phase 7 ships TWO atlas regenerations (7.A.1 = 1457 bytes, 7.A.2a = 1610 bytes) — the deterministic-encoder discipline + cross-OS matrix from Phase 4.B handles both. Phase 7.A.2b ships ZERO atlas changes; the atlas binary at ATLAS_DIGEST 8ca99389… from Phase 7.A.2a is unchanged.
next_phase: Phase 7.B — live GitHub Pages deployment + cross-runtime Playwright (chromium/firefox/webkit) asserting the buy → descend → defeat-boss scripted sequence produces WIN_DIGEST end-to-end + Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix re-running against the 1610-byte atlas binary (unchanged from Phase 7.A.2a). Phase 7.B may need a new __SIM_WIN_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ window flag in src/main.ts mirroring the existing __SIM_FINAL_STATE_HASH__ pattern, plus a corresponding diagnostic.spec.ts test that asserts equality with the WIN_DIGEST literal already mirrored at the top of the file. CI fails if the checked-in atlas is stale per the existing git diff --exit-code -- assets/ gate.
